text: Wore this at -12 degrees in Harbin for a full day, stayed completely warm. The duck down is seriously effective.
The navy blue is a deep rich color, not faded or washed out. Looks exactly like the photo.
Runs slightly small. I'm 172cm and usually M, had to size up to L for comfortable layering.
Not warm enough below -15. Fine for -5 to -10 degrees, but anything colder and you'll feel it.
Outer shell is smooth and windproof. No crinkly sound when moving, looks very premium.
Hood is generous and covers the face well in wind. The drawstring actually works properly.
Stayed completely dry in heavy rain for 45 minutes. This coat is genuinely waterproof, not just water resistant.
The khaki is a warm classic tan, not yellow. Very elegant and professional looking.
Warm down to about 5 degrees with the removable wool lining. Below that you'll need a sweater underneath.
One button came loose after two weeks. Quality of buttons doesn't match the coat's price.
I'm 168cm and the M hits just below the knee. Perfect coverage and great proportions.
Great for autumn office wear. Smart enough for meetings, comfortable enough for the commute.
I have rosacea and the merino collar doesn't irritate my neck at all, even after hours of wear. Incredibly soft.
The ivory white is clean and bright. Very classic and easy to style with anything.
Warm enough for 0 to 10 degree indoor environments. Perfect for cold offices.
Shrunk slightly after first wash despite following care instructions. Use cold water and lay flat to dry.
My skin reacts to most wool, but extra-fine merino is completely fine. No itching whatsoever.
Runs slim. Size up one if you prefer a relaxed fit or plan to layer underneath.
The cashmere is unbelievably soft. I have sensitive skin and this feels like nothing against my neck.
The camel brown is a warm honey tone, slightly deeper than the photo but still beautiful.
Keeps my neck surprisingly warm in 0 to 5 degree weather. The cashmere-silk blend traps heat effectively.
The fringe edges are neat and don't tangle. Holds its shape well after multiple wears.
Starting to pill after 6 uses. Cashmere needs careful hand washing in cool water.
Very lightweight for how warm it is. Doesn't add bulk under a coat.
The softest fleece I've owned. My eczema-prone skin has zero reaction, even on bare arms.
Heather grey looks exactly as shown. Very versatile, goes with everything.
Good for 5 to 15 degree weather. Too warm indoors in heated buildings, perfect for outdoor autumn use.
Pilling appeared after 6 washes on gentle cycle. Disappointing for a fleece at this price.
Runs true to size. I'm 165cm 60kg and the M fits perfectly, relaxed without being baggy.
The zip glides smoothly and the pocket opening is wide enough to be actually useful.
The cotton is breathable and stays fresh all day. No synthetic smell or stuffiness.
Crisp white, exactly as shown. Resists yellowing better than my previous white shirts.
Perfect for sensitive skin. 100% cotton, no dyes or finishes that irritate.
Slim fit runs small in the shoulders. Size up if you have broader shoulders.
Comfortable at 15 to 25 degrees. Gets cold below that without a layer over it.
Machine washable and comes out wrinkle-free on a warm cycle. Very practical.
Wore under ski gear at -15 degrees and stayed completely warm and dry. Excellent moisture wicking.
Charcoal grey is a dark neutral, completely invisible under clothes. Great as a base layer.
Flatlock seams make a real difference on long days. No rubbing or chafing even on sensitive skin.
Slightly itchy for first few wears, but completely softened after 3 washes.
Runs true to size. Form fitting but stretchy enough to move freely in.
Stays dry during winter runs. Wicks moisture away fast and doesn't feel damp against skin.
Tested in strong winds at the coast. Completely windproof, no cold air getting through.
Forest green is a deep muted tone, not bright or flashy. Very outdoorsy and versatile.
Water resistant, not waterproof. Fine in light drizzle but soaks through in heavy rain.
The stretch panels at the elbows allow a full range of motion for climbing. Very practical design.
Warm enough as a single layer down to about 5 degrees. Light enough to pack easily.
Skin doesn't react to the inner lining at all. Very smooth and comfortable against bare arms.
Sky blue is accurate to the listing. Clean and fresh looking for summer.
Linen is naturally breathable. Stayed cool in 35 degree humid weather without sweating through.
Wrinkles a lot. That's normal for linen but steam before wearing if you want a crisp look.
Very gentle on skin. No synthetic fibers, no irritation, even in hot weather when skin is sensitive.
Relaxed fit is roomy without being shapeless. Works well for tall and shorter builds.
Gets softer with each wash. After 5 washes it drapes beautifully.
The matte black looks sharp. No shiny finish, very minimal and clean.
Warm enough as a vest down to about 5 degrees when worn over a mid-layer.
Packs into its own pocket in about 10 seconds. Very compact for travel.
Slim fit is quite fitted. Anyone between sizes should size up.
The synthetic fill doesn't trigger my down allergy. Great alternative for people sensitive to feathers.
Interior security pocket is large enough for a passport. Very useful for travel.
Indigo blue is a classic medium blue, no greenish or purple tint. Looks great.
The 2% elastane means these actually bend at the knee without pulling. Real improvement over regular denim.
Runs slightly slim in the thighs. Anyone with muscular legs should size up.
Comfortable at any temperature with layering. The cotton breathes well and doesn't trap heat.
Seams are reinforced properly. No unraveling after multiple machine washes.
Belt loops are wide enough for most belts, including the thicker ones.
The softest sleepwear I have owned. Bamboo fabric feels cool and silky against skin all night.
Perfect for people with eczema. Non-irritating, breathable, and stays cool even in warm rooms.
Sage green is a muted earthy tone, slightly grey-green. Very calming and not at all garish.
Too cool for winter without heating on. Best for spring, autumn or air-conditioned rooms.
Runs slightly large. I'm 163cm 55kg and the S fits with a little extra room.
Wrinkles easily. Needs a quick steam after drying. Minor issue for such comfortable fabric.
Zero foot odor even after a 10km run. Merino wool manages moisture and smell incredibly well.
My daughter has a wool allergy but tolerates these fine. Fine merino is hypoallergenic for most.
Warm in autumn but not bulky in shoes. Perfect for transitional weather between 5 and 15 degrees.
Flat toe seam makes a real difference. No irritation on my bunion-prone toes even on long walks.
Hole appeared at the heel after 2 months of daily wear. Durability is the weak point.
The white and grey mix stays white after many washes. No yellowing.
Rust orange is a deep burnt tone, not bright at all. Very autumn and earthy looking.
Works perfectly as a light outer layer down to 12 degrees. Too thin below that without layering.
Corduroy is soft and smooth. No irritation at all against my arms or neck.
Oversized enough to work as a jacket over a t-shirt, smart enough to wear open like a blazer.
Runs oversized as intended. The M suits anyone from 165 to 175cm for a relaxed look.
Chest pockets are functional and actually close. Button flaps don't come undone accidentally.
Wore in -18 degree weather in northeast China. Stayed completely warm without anything underneath.
Oyster white is a very clean off-white, slightly warm-toned. Looks luxurious and minimal.
Ankle length gives maximum warmth. Your whole body is covered even when sitting.
800-fill goose down is noticeably warmer than standard 600-fill coats. Worth the price difference.
The concealed zip is fiddly. Takes practice to open and close smoothly.
White color shows dirt easily. Clean regularly and spot-treat immediately for best results.
